---
title: "LLM Agents: A Survey of Architectures & Designs (2022–2026)"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# LLM Agents: A Survey (2022–2026)

A thorough review of the landscape of LLM-based agent architectures, designs, and the evolving ecosystem — covering papers, preprints, frameworks, blog posts, and open-source tools.

*Compiled March 2026 · [View on GitHub](https://github.com/johnplake/llm-agents-survey)*
:::

## What Is This?

This survey maps the landscape of **Large Language Model (LLM) based agents** — systems where LLMs serve not just as text generators, but as reasoning engines that can plan, use tools, access memory, and take actions in the world.

From 2022 to 2026, the field has exploded. This guide aims to be a **navigable map**: organized by theme, covering both the academic literature and the practitioner ecosystem.

::: {.stats-bar}
::: {.stat-item}
::: {.stat-number}
4+
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Years Covered
:::
:::
::: {.stat-item}
::: {.stat-number}
300+
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Papers & Resources
:::
:::
::: {.stat-item}
::: {.stat-number}
8
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Major Themes
:::
:::
::: {.stat-item}
::: {.stat-number}
2022–2026
:::
::: {.stat-label}
Time Range
:::
:::
:::

## Navigate the Survey

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🗺️
:::
### [Taxonomy & Framework](taxonomy.qmd)
A conceptual map of the field: how to decompose and categorize LLM agents. The core components, design dimensions, and how the major architectures fit together.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🏛️
:::
### [Foundations (2022–2023)](foundations.qmd)
The papers and ideas that established the field. ReAct, Toolformer, HuggingGPT, WebGPT, and the early agent taxonomies.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🧠
:::
### [Reasoning & Planning](reasoning.qmd)
How agents think: Chain-of-Thought, Tree of Thoughts, Reflexion, search-based planning, task decomposition, and self-improvement.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🤝
:::
### [Multi-Agent Systems](multiagent.qmd)
Multiple agents working together: CAMEL, AutoGen, MetaGPT, ChatDev, role specialization, orchestration patterns, and evaluation benchmarks.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🧰
:::
### [Memory, Tools & Actions](memory-tools.qmd)
How agents remember and act: memory architectures, tool use (Toolformer, function calling), action spaces, retrieval-augmented agents, and computer use.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🚀
:::
### [2024–2026: Frontier](recent.qmd)
The leading edge: coding agents (SWE-agent, Devin), agentic products (Operator, Computer Use), MCP, o1/o3 implications, safety, and where things are heading.
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
📊
:::
### [Topics: Evaluation, Safety, Science](topics.qmd)
Cross-cutting themes with their own pages: agent benchmarks (SWE-bench, METR, WebArena), safety and alignment (prompt injection, trust, reversibility), and science-specific agents (FutureHouse, Google AI Co-Scientist).
:::

::: {.survey-card}
::: {.card-icon}
🛠️
:::
### [Community & Independent Agents](community.qmd)
OpenClaw, Pi, Strix, crow-cli, Agno, Orchestra, TapeAgents, AgentFS, stereOS, and the grassroots builder ecosystem.
:::

:::

## How to Use This Survey

- **New to agents?** Start with [Taxonomy](taxonomy.qmd), then [Foundations](foundations.qmd)
- **Researcher?** Each section has structured paper entries with links and summaries
- **Practitioner?** Check [Multi-Agent Systems](multiagent.qmd) for frameworks and [2024–2026](recent.qmd) for the current ecosystem
- **Looking for resources?** See [Resources & Links](resources.qmd) for a curated reading list

## Scope & Methodology

This survey covers:

- **Papers & preprints** (arXiv, ACL, NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, etc.)
- **Technical blog posts** from researchers and practitioners
- **Open-source frameworks and tools** (GitHub)
- **Time range:** 2022 through March 2026

The focus is on **LLM-based agents** — systems using large language models as a core reasoning component. We touch on embodied/robotics agents when they are directly relevant, but the primary focus is on digital, language-model-centric work.

---

*This survey was compiled in March 2026. The field moves fast — if you spot a missing reference, [open an issue](https://github.com/johnplake/llm-agents-survey/issues).*
